channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: New York’s Haitian Community Reacts to Supreme Court’s Immigration Decision
item.description: Uncertainty gripped the Haitian community in New York after the Supreme Court ruled that the Trump administration could end Temporary Protected Status.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Shannon Stapleton/Reuters

item.title: Intel’s Chip Business Shows Signs of Life After Years of Struggle
item.description: It is the centerpiece of President Trump’s drive to make more chips in the United States, but the company still has a long way to go before it can be called a complete turnaround.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle

media:content.media:credit: Lincoln Agnew

item.title: How a Niche Technology Became a Choke Point for A.I.
item.description: Advanced chip packaging, which boosts computing power for artificial intelligence, has made the United States more reliant on Taiwan than ever.
item.dc:creator: Don Clark and Gabriela Bhaskar

media:content.media:description: A silicon wafer reflecting Subramanian Iyer, a specialist at the University of California, Los Angeles, in a technology called advanced chip packaging.

item.title: The High School Pipeline to South Korea’s Chip-Making Fortunes
item.description: Huge memory-chip profits from the global A.I. boom have increased interest in semiconductor factory work. But behind the hype are uncertain job prospects.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim

media:content.media:credit: Tina Hsu for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A class about diffusion equipment operation taught by Kang Soo Jin.

item.title: Cuando tengas un día caótico, deja que esta canción te de armonía
item.description: La obra maestra de Stephen Sondheim, ‘Sunday in the Park With George’, tiene una canción capaz de poner orden en medio del caos. Escúchala para descubrir cómo lo consiguió.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.category: Sunday in the Park with George (Play)

item.title: Flight of the Conchords y el riesgo de los reencuentros
item.description: A Bret McKenzie y Jemaine Clement se les pregunta habitualmente si volverían a interpretar sus sátiras musicales. Siempre dijeron que no, hasta este año. ¿Qué cambió?
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

media:content.media:credit: Chantal Anderson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Clement said, “One of my favorite things to do was when someone heckled us, we talk about why that hurt our feelings.”

item.title: After Almost 50 Years, Etan Patz’s Father Is Relieved to Have Closure
item.description: In a rare interview, Stanley Patz said he was relieved not to have to return to court and relive the day of his son’s disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

item.title: These Unpaid Interns Want $32 an Hour. And Health Insurance.
item.description: A group of government interns is campaigning for a paid wage, reigniting a debate over ethics and opportunity in one of the world’s most expensive cities.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mina Farahmand and Ashley Cerda had unpaid internships with the New York City Council.

item.title: Why Everyone in This Irish Pub Is Rooting for Cape Verde’s Soccer Team
item.description: Cape Verde, a tiny African nation in its first World Cup, has become the underdog darling of the tournament. The Irish have a personal reason to join the bandwagon.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cape Verde fans and fans of the Republic of Ireland have joined in supporting Roberto Lopes, a defender for Cape Verde with Irish heritage who is known as Pico.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 26, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘Strung’ Review: A Tease of a Thriller
item.description: Chloe Bailey, Lynn Whitfield and Lucien Laviscount have delivered more of a camp classic than a taut psychological film.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: Stocks in Asia Tumble as A.I. Uncertainty Jolts Tech Shares
item.description: Stock markets across Asia tumbled, as investors continue to wrestle with whether the boom in artificial intelligence spending is overdone or just beginning.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Greg Baker/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Late Night Grades the Attractions at the Great American State Fair
item.description: The fair has everything, Jimmy Fallon said, including “a Trump approval rating roller coaster, which has the biggest drop in history.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: It’s Time to Put This Catholic Teaching to Rest
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical takes on a major flaw of Catholic just war theory: its use as a fig leaf for attacking an enemy.
item.dc:creator: James V. Grimaldi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Juan Naharro Gimenez/Getty Images

item.title: In London, You Can Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday From the British Side
item.description: Britain may have lost the Revolutionary War, but London still holds the blueprints of the American Revolution. Follow in the founders’ footsteps at these notable places.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Jeremie Souteyrat

media:content.media:description: Inside Middle Temple Hall, a 16th-century, wood-paneled hall where legal scholars have long gathered.

item.title: A $2.5 Billion Whodunit: The Hack That Dented the U.K. Economy
item.description: A loose collective of cybercriminals initially took credit for crippling Jaguar Land Rover last year. Investigators now see Russian hands behind the ransomware attack.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman, Jane Bradley, Dustin Volz and Michael Schwirtz

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Land Rovers being transported from the Jaguar Land Rover factory in Solihull, England, last year.

item.title: Quote of the Day: It’s Easy to See Why the World’s Tango Lovers Don’t Cry for Turkey
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, June 25, 20206.

item.title: Climate Change Fueling Europe’s Ferocious Heat Wave, Scientists Find
item.description: A scientific analysis concluded that such high temperatures, across so much of the continent, would “not have been possible” without global warming.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Zhong

media:content.media:credit: Christophe Archambault/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A shady passageway on Tuesday in Bordeaux, France, where temperatures reached 108 degrees Fahrenheit, or roughly 42 Celsius.

item.title: I Thought Divorce Meant Walking Away from the Past
item.description: My marriage had a lot of good that was hard for me to forget. I’m glad I couldn’t.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Gregg

media:content.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: Two Introverts Happen to Get Married During the Knicks Parade
item.description: Jonathan Hicks and Caitlin Remington intended to have a low-key wedding at City Hall. Then the Ticker-tape parade was scheduled.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: World Cups Fans in Myanmar Boycott Junta-Linked Broadcasts
item.description: In Myanmar, games are being broadcast by a company that is co-owned by the military, which usurped power five years ago. Some fans are trying other ways to watch.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock

media:content.media:credit: Catherine Ivill — AMA/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An informal boycott of official World Cup broadcasts has spread across Myanmar.

item.title: Years After High School, Their Love Story Began
item.description: A chance reunion at a sports bar led Cailin Cook and Peri Ipiotis to a first date, a pandemic-era romance and a wedding in Montauk.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: They Wanted a New York Wedding. They Got a Very New York Ending.
item.description: Devin McGinley and Lauren Birkhold celebrated their marriage along with the Knicks’ N.B.A championship.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

item.title: For Two Met Opera Performers, a Few Multicolored Clown Wigs Were a Green Flag
item.description: Carla Jablonski and Arthur Lazalde had their first date on March 10, 2020. Days later, as the coronavirus forced cancellations at the Met, they found themselves spending a lot more time together.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: Couple No. 16 Marries at Shakespeare in the Park
item.description: Dr. David Witzel and Paolo Valore, one of 33 couples who will have wed or renewed vows during “Romeo and Juliet,” made even the actors emotional.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: The Maine Town That Actually Wants a Data Center
item.description: This year, Maine nearly became the first state to pass a statewide moratorium on new data centers. But before the law could take effect, supporters of an A.I. data center project in the small town of Jay rallied to fight the ban — and won. So why do residents there want one? We traveled to Jay to find out.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Raff, Zach Caldwell and Ben Laffin

item.title: The Aftermath of Venezuela’s Earthquakes
item.description: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez, a photographer for The New York Times, describes the scene on the ground after the Venezuelan earthquakes.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez, Leila Medina and Estelle Caswell

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 26, 2026
item.description: Spencer Leach returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Brian Harkin for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Disclaimer: No knowledge of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, nor the Renaissance artists they’re named after, are required to solve this puzzle.

item.title: California Will Vote on a Billionaire Tax. Billionaires Aren’t Happy.
item.description: The wealthy are fighting back with two ballot issues that could nullify the measure in November. The battle reflects a broader tension as American politics contends with deep inequality.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: California voters will confront ballot issues designed to cancel one another.

item.title: ‘Love Island USA’ Kicks Out Another Contestant for Racial Slur
item.description: After Alannah Keyser made her debut on Sunday, viewers quickly found a social media video in which she uses a slur while singing.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Peacock
media:content.media:description: Alannah Keyser joined “Love Island USA” on Sunday.

item.title: Venezuela busca sobrevivientes, y un milagro, tras un devastador doblete sísmico
item.description: Dos terremotos en rápida sucesión han llevado al país latinoamericano al límite de su resistencia.
item.dc:creator: María Victoria Fermín, Anatoly Kurmanaev, Julie Turkewitz, Isayen Herrera and Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Búsqueda de sobrevivientes el jueves entre los escombros de un edificio en Catia La Mar, Venezuela, que se derrumbó un día antes durante un terremoto.

item.title: Trump Issues Order to Reduce Pesticides in Food as Kennedy Allies Fume
item.description: The order, which calls for studying the health risks of pesticides in the food supply, does not involve new federal funding, and does not call for regulations or legislation.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump held a Rose Garden dinner with American farmers on Thursday, the same day he issued an executive order aimed at reducing pesticides in the food supply.

item.title: Authorities in Kenya Shut Down Nairobi to Block Annual Protest
item.description: Highways were blocked off, and the security forces were deployed to the streets of Kenya’s capital on Thursday ahead of a protest commemorating the more than 60 people killed during the protests on June 25, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:credit: Luis Tato/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Protesters being arrested outside Parliament buildings in Nairobi on Thursday.

item.title: ¿Cuánto tiempo puede sobrevivir una persona bajo los escombros?
item.description: Depende del agua, del aire y de lo rápido que los equipos de rescate puedan encontrar a la gente
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Equipos de rescate recorriendo un edificio que colapsó durante los terremotos en Venezuela.

item.title: Mamdani’s Rent Freeze Is Approved by New York City Board
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s pledge to freeze rents for rent-stabilized apartments became a centerpiece of his campaign. Six months into his first year, a city panel delivered.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: As New York City’s housing crisis has intensified, tenant advocates have pushed for a rent freeze, while landlords have argued it would result in more poorly maintained buildings.

item.title: Johnson Says He Will Send Housing Bill to Trump
item.description: After meeting with the president, the speaker said he would send him a housing bill that Mr. Trump declined to sign this week. There was no word on whether he would sign it.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson did not say on Thursday whether President Trump had promised to sign the housing bill or whether the two had even discussed it.

item.title: La Guaira: una ciudad venezolana marcada por la tragedia
item.description: Los mortales deslaves de 1999 asestaron golpes duraderos a la empobrecida La Guaira. La respuesta de la presidenta en funciones, Delcy Rodríguez, podría ser un indicador de su futuro político.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev

item.title: Blakeman Suggests Lander, a Jewish Politician, Would Have Aided Nazis
item.description: Brad Lander, a Democrat who won his House primary in New York, told voters to reject the “bigotry” of Bruce Blakeman, a Republican candidate for governor who is also Jewish.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bruce Blakeman, the Nassau County executive, said Brad Lander was antisemitic.

item.title: How Trump Wants to Upend Voting in America
item.description: President Trump is demanding that Congress pass the SAVE America Act to change American elections. Our national politics reporter Nick Corasaniti looks at what’s in it.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Laura Bult, Jon Miller, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Gilad Thaler

item.title: Trump Plans to Name Adam Candeub as Antitrust Chief in Justice Dept.
item.description: President Trump plans to nominate a tech critic and telecommunications lawyer, Adam Candeub, to lead the division.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: If confirmed by the Senate, Adam Candeub, the general counsel at the Federal Communications Commission, would lead the antitrust division at Department of Justice, above.

item.title: Obama Says He Occupies a ‘Suite’ in Trump’s Head
item.description: Former President Barack Obama says President Trump has an obsession with him and that Mr. Trump “knows better” than to say “crazy stuff” to his face.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: Obama, Barack H, Presidential Library and Museum

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Former President Barack Obama at the opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago last week.

item.title: ‘Little Brother’ Review: Just the Two of Us
item.description: John Cena and Eric André star in this crude, but funny, movie about a real estate agent who encounters a blast from the past.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: Congo Ebola Crisis: Contact Tracing Is Dangerously Behind, Officials Warn
item.description: Most of the people testing positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo are not on health workers’ radar, suggesting that contact tracing is lagging dangerously behind.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Stephanie Nolen

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A community health worker taking the temperature of a man whose son is being treated for the virus near the city of Bunia in Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: How Long Can a Person Survive Under Rubble?
item.description: It depends on water, air and how quickly rescuers are able to find people.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rescue teams walking through a building that collapsed during the earthquakes in Venezuela.

item.title: He Was Deported Back to Venezuela and Started Anew. Then the Quakes Hit.
item.description: Venezuelans in South Florida were frantically trying to track down friends and relatives after two earthquakes in Venezuela. Jorge Erazo was among the missing.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Civilians and emergency medical workers looking for survivors in the rubble of a building that collapsed during an earthquake in Catia La Mar, Venezuela.

item.title: Military Leaders Sought Flu Vaccination Program Weeks Before Outbreak Hit Base
item.description: Officials anticipated a problem soon after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lifted the vaccine requirement in April.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Lackland Air Force Base (San Antonio, Tex)

media:content.media:credit: Lynsey Addario for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Marine recruits during basic training at Parris Island in South Carolina in 2019.

item.title: Who Is Ahead in Florida's 7th Congressional District?
item.description: Track the latest polls in Florida's 7th Congressional District.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Footage From Before and After Venezuelan Quakes Shows Scope of Damage
item.description: At least 235 people were dead and more than 4,300 people were injured after two back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, according to the country’s government. Before-and-after images show the scope of the damage.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper, Daniel Fetherston and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: Who Is Ahead in Arizona's 2nd Congressional District?
item.description: Track the latest polls in Arizona's 2nd Congressional District.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Terremotos en Venezuela: la búsqueda de sobrevivientes se intensifica
item.description: Los sismos consecutivos han dejado al menos 235 muertos y más de 4300 heridos, según las autoridades. En La Guaira se calcula que hay cientos de personas atrapadas entre los escombros. Se espera que el número de víctimas aumente.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los primeros rescatistas miran hacia un edificio parcialmente colapsado en Catia La Mar, Venezuela.
